title: madhuca pallida
intro: Madhuca pallida, also known as the white butter tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of India and Sri Lanka. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bark and leaves of the Madhuca pallida tree have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Madhuca pallida include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
